PR surface: Source +360, Tests +490, Docs +35. Total +885 across 29 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a new feature, and current main lacks the setup metadata and allowSetupHooks surfaces.

commands.author: zhangguiping-xydt

target.title: fix #80213: [Feature]: Skill author-defined setup hook (run skill-supplied script on install/update)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #80213.

- Keep skill-defined setup hooks disabled by default for ClawHub, upload, source, and gateway installer flows.
- Add an explicit `allowSetupHooks` / `--allow-setup-hooks` opt-in through the gateway protocol, gateway handlers, CLI, and install/update lifecycle paths.
- Document setup hook trust, host-owned timeout, failure, minimal hook env, and staging semantics, with regression coverage for default-skip and explicit opt-in behavior.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Skill packages can declare `metadata.openclaw.setup.script`, but OpenClaw no longer runs downloaded or installed skill setup hooks by default. Trusted admin/gateway/CLI callers must explicitly opt in, and the opt-in path confines setup scripts while giving hooks only minimal host context.
- **Real environment tested:** Existing PR repair worktree `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-80266`; Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64; Node 22.22.0; repository pnpm/tooling; evidence under `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the gateway proof script with `pnpm exec node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/gateway-skills-install-setup-proof.ts"`; reran current-head frontmatter setup metadata tests, setup/install lifecycle tests, CLI/gateway/upload tests, production and test type checks, changed-file formatting, docs formatting, diff whitespace, public `setup.timeoutMs` contract search, and non-doc/lockfile diff-size count.
- **Evidence after fix:** `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/gateway-skills-install-setup-proof-current-head.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/gateway-skills-install-setup-proof-result.json`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/vitest-setup-env-gateway-current-head.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/timeout-contract-verification-current-head.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/tsgo-core-prod-current-head.log`; `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/tsgo-core-test-current-head-after-rebase.log`; earlier broader proof logs remain in `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80266-evidence/verification-summary.md`.
- **Observed result after fix:** Current-head gateway proof reports `defaultGateSkippedHook: true`, `explicitOptInHookRan: true`, `hookKind: "install"`, `configuredEnvBlocked: true`, `gatewayTokenBlocked: true`, and `nodeOptionsBlocked: true`; setup hook env is limited to host `PATH`, `SKILL_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_HOOK_KIND`. Timeout-contract verification passed frontmatter setup metadata coverage (16 tests), lifecycle setup/install coverage (65 tests), type checking, formatting, docs formatting, `git diff --check`, and confirmed no public `setup.timeoutMs` contract references. Focused CLI/gateway/upload coverage passed 5 files / 92 tests. Non-doc/lockfile diff size is 726 changed lines, below the `size: XL` threshold of 1000.
- **What was not tested:** A live published ClawHub skill containing setup metadata was not installed from the public registry. This repair uses the real local gateway upload install path plus lifecycle/CLI/gateway handler tests, and it keeps live ClawHub setup execution behind explicit opt-in rather than default autorun.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium-high for updating this existing PR. The code path that previously changed the trust boundary is now explicitly gated, the public gateway/CLI opt-in is wired, and local gateway/lifecycle/CLI evidence proves default-skip and opt-in behavior. Confidence is not marked high only because a live published ClawHub registry install was not available in this environment.
- **Patch quality notes:** The warnings around timeout/fallback/catch handling are part of the new setup-hook execution boundary: setup scripts are external process/file inputs, so the runner must report parse/read/execute failures, keep timeout policy host-owned, and support `sh` for non-executable shell scripts. These are not used to mask the trust-boundary issue; the root behavior change is the explicit opt-in gate across ClawHub, upload, source, gateway, and CLI entry points. The test-only type narrowing in ClawHub assertions checks whether the install transaction received an `afterInstall` callback without changing production types.
- **Target test file:** `src/skills/lifecycle/setup.test.ts`, `src/skills/lifecycle/archive-install.test.ts`, `src/skills/lifecycle/install.test.ts`, `src/skills/lifecycle/source-install.test.ts`, `src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts`, `src/skills/loading/frontmatter.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/skills-upload.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts`, `src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts`

## Why it matters / User impact

Skill authors requested a setup hook so installed skills can bootstrap local files or dependencies. Without this patch, OpenClaw either cannot run that declared setup path at install/update time or risks doing so implicitly in downloaded skill flows. The user-visible behavior after this patch is deliberate: normal installs/updates remain safe by default, while trusted CLI/gateway callers can opt in when they intentionally accept the local script execution boundary.

## Public contract and compatibility scope

- **Canonical scope:** `metadata.openclaw.setup.script` is the only skill-authored setup metadata in this PR, and `allowSetupHooks` / `--allow-setup-hooks` is the caller/admin opt-in that controls execution. `setup.timeoutMs` is intentionally not part of the public SKILL.md contract; setup timeout policy remains host-owned.
- **Default behavior:** setup hooks are parsed but not executed unless the caller opts in, so existing install/update flows do not start running downloaded scripts by default.
- **Related PR scan:** ClawSweeper flagged a competing draft admin-only plugin/RPC implementation and a published ClawHub plugin. This branch keeps the core install/update opt-in path under review and leaves the final product boundary choice to maintainers.
- **Out of scope:** no live public ClawHub registry setup-hook install was exercised here, no protocol version bump is made, no default autorun path is added, and this PR does not decide whether a plugin/RPC-only design should supersede the core path.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: compatibility`, `merge-risk: security-boundary`, plus availability/session-state/message-delivery/auth-provider-adjacent signals from gateway and install/update paths.
- **Risk explanation:** The PR adds a public setup metadata shape and an opt-in execution path for skill-supplied scripts on the gateway/CLI host. That can affect install/update failure behavior, local process execution, minimal hook env semantics, gateway handler contracts, and docs/schema expectations.
- **Why acceptable:** The risky behavior is disabled by default, requires an explicit opt-in on each trusted caller path, uses realpath confinement for setup scripts, keeps setup timeout policy host-owned, and constructs setup child env from only `PATH`, `SKILL_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_HOOK_KIND` through the shared host exec sanitizer.

## Regression Test Plan

- `pnpm exec node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/gateway-skills-install-setup-proof.ts"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --reporter verbose src/skills/loading/frontmatter.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts --reporter verbose src/skills/lifecycle/source-install.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/setup.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/install.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/archive-install.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --reporter verbose src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills-upload.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-current-head.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-current-head.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.ts src/skills/lifecycle/archive-install.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/archive-install.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts src/skills/lifecycle/install.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/install.ts src/skills/lifecycle/setup.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/setup.ts src/skills/lifecycle/source-install.ts src/skills/lifecycle/upload-install.ts src/skills/loading/frontmatter.test.ts`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The original setup-hook implementation exposed a new executable skill metadata surface while enabling the ClawHub install/update path to run downloaded setup scripts by default. At the same time, the public gateway/CLI contract did not consistently expose one deliberate trust-boundary opt-in for the workflow described by the issue, so the risky default path and the intended admin-controlled path diverged.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch makes the trust decision explicit and consistent at the source of the behavior. Setup hooks remain parsed and executable only after the caller opts in through `allowSetupHooks` or `--allow-setup-hooks`; that same decision is propagated through gateway protocol validation, gateway handlers, CLI commands, ClawHub install/update, upload installs, source installs, and local installer execution. The added docs and tests cover both sides of the invariant: default installs/updates do not run setup hooks, and trusted opt-in installs/updates run them with realpath confinement plus minimal hook env.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 (`status: waiting on author`):** This refresh updates the branch with current-head proof; the label itself is bot/maintainer-owned.
- **RF-002 (OpenGrep skill env host injection):** Addressed in current head. Setup hooks no longer consume configured skill env or `requires.env` values at all; they build child env from only `PATH`, `SKILL_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_HOOK_KIND` through `sanitizeHostExecEnv`, while normal skill runtime env overrides keep the shared host-env sanitizer hardening.
- **RF-003 (new public metadata / CLI / gateway contract):** Maintainer decision required. This refresh narrows the skill-authored metadata to `metadata.openclaw.setup.script` only, intentionally leaves setup timeout policy host-owned, and keeps the remaining execution contract explicit, documented, and opt-in rather than adding a hidden default execution path.
- **RF-004 (gateway-host script execution trust boundary):** Maintainer security-boundary approval required. The automated repair keeps execution disabled by default and confines env/path/script handling; it does not hide that this is a deliberate trust-boundary change.
- **RF-005 (competing plugin/RPC boundary):** Maintainer product-boundary decision required. This PR preserves the core install/update opt-in implementation because that is the branch under review.
- **RF-006 (remaining action is maintainer API/security approval):** Confirmed. No narrower automated code repair is available beyond the explicit opt-in and sanitizer proof already included.
- **RF-007 (real behavior proof):** Current-head proof is recorded in `gateway-skills-install-setup-proof-current-head.log`, `gateway-skills-install-setup-proof-result.json`, and `timeout-contract-verification-current-head.log`.
- **RF-008 (reviewer real behavior proof request):** Addressed with local gateway upload/install proof plus current-head lifecycle, CLI, gateway, typecheck, format, docs, and public-contract-search verification. A live published ClawHub registry skill with setup metadata was still not available in this environment and remains disclosed above.
- **RF-009 (real behavior proof):** Addressed with current-head real gateway upload/install proof showing default setup hooks are skipped, explicit opt-in runs the hook, and configured/sensitive env is blocked; supporting Vitest, typecheck, format, docs, diff-check, and size-count evidence is listed in the Real behavior proof section.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
